queue_item.action: Prove Event Timing and Long Animation Frame summaries came from the same navigation, clock domain, and sufficiently overlapping drained observation window before fusing them.

progress.finding: A shared replay label is insufficient evidence of shared observation scope. Cross-observer fusion must fail closed unless both summaries declare the same navigation and time-origin token, have drained buffers with zero known dropped entries, and cover a strongly overlapping bounded interval.
progress.verification: 6 deterministic Node.js tests passed locally before commit.
progress.privacy_boundary: Output contains opaque replay, navigation, and time-origin tokens and aggregate interval bounds only; raw entries, DOM targets, selectors, event names, script attribution, page text, private chat, health data, and credentials are excluded.
progress.uncertainty: The 0.9 overlap threshold is a project heuristic rather than a standards threshold; same-window integrity prevents cross-window fusion but does not prove causation; target-browser support remains unverified.

queue_item.handoff: Vercel Studio should generate one opaque navigation identity and one opaque time-origin token per loaded document, record observer start and drain boundaries for R-009 and R-010, run this validator, and pass only window_integrity=true summaries into R-011.

coordination.reason: Append-only queue update preserves concurrent team state and the canonical queue's historical snapshot.
